seo_description: Current time in Cornwall, UK. Live GMT/BST clock — UTC+0 standard, UTC+1 British Summer Time. Cornwall time vs New York, London, Paris, Dublin.

faq.question: What time is it in Cornwall right now?
faq.answer: Cornwall operates on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT/UTC+0) during winter and British Summer Time (BST/UTC+1) from late March to late October. Cornwall is part of the United Kingdom and follows the same time zone as London, Edinburgh, Cardiff, and Dublin. To see the exact current time in Cornwall with live seconds, use our real-time clock above.

faq.question: What time zone is Cornwall in?
faq.answer: Cornwall is in the Greenwich Mean Time zone (GMT/UTC+0 in winter, BST/UTC+1 in summer), identical to the rest of England, Wales, and Scotland. Despite being the UK's most south-westerly peninsula, Cornwall does not have its own time zone — it follows UK national time. Some locals have historically campaigned for a 'Cornish Time' zone aligned with Central European Time, but no official change has been made.

faq.question: Does Cornwall observe Daylight Saving Time?
faq.answer: Yes. Cornwall, as part of the UK, observes British Summer Time (BST). Clocks spring forward one hour on the last Sunday in March at 1:00 AM GMT (becoming 2:00 AM BST/UTC+1), and fall back one hour on the last Sunday in October at 2:00 AM BST (becoming 1:00 AM GMT/UTC+0). The longer summer evenings are particularly valued in Cornwall, where outdoor tourism and beach activities dominate the visitor economy.

faq.question: Is Cornwall in the same time zone as London?
faq.answer: Yes. Cornwall is in exactly the same time zone as London — GMT in winter and BST in summer. There is no time difference between Penzance, Newquay, Falmouth, Truro, or any other Cornish town and London. The UK uses a single time zone across the entire mainland, so Cornwall's westernmost location (Land's End) does not affect its clock.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Cornwall and New York?
faq.answer: Cornwall is 5 hours ahead of New York during US Eastern Standard Time (EST/UTC-5), and 5 hours ahead during EDT (UTC-4) as well, since UK DST transitions occur around the same time as US DST. When it is 9:00 AM in New York, it is 2:00 PM in Cornwall. The 5-hour difference is consistent year-round, making transatlantic scheduling between Cornwall and the US East Coast straightforward.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Cornwall and Paris?
faq.answer: Cornwall (GMT/BST) is always 1 hour behind Paris (CET/CEST). When it is 12:00 PM in Paris, it is 11:00 AM in Cornwall. Both regions observe DST on the same Sunday in March and October, so the 1-hour gap remains constant throughout the year. This makes Cornwall effectively in the same time corridor as Ireland and Portugal, rather than continental Europe.

faq.question: What is the best time to visit Cornwall?
faq.answer: The best time to visit Cornwall is from May to September, during British Summer Time (BST/UTC+1). July and August are the peak tourist months, with average temperatures of 18–20°C and long evenings — sunset in Newquay can be as late as 9:30 PM BST in June. The spring shoulder season (May–June) and autumn (September–October) offer fewer crowds and mild weather ideal for coastal walking, surfing, and cycling. Winter brings dramatic Atlantic storms popular with storm-chasers but fewer amenities.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Cornwall?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Cornwall run 9:00 AM–5:00 PM GMT/BST Monday to Friday, matching the rest of England. Retail shops in towns like Truro, Falmouth, and St Ives typically open 9:00 AM–5:30 PM. Tourist season (June–August) sees many attractions, restaurants, and shops extending hours to 8:00 PM or later. Surf schools and outdoor activity providers in Newquay often begin sessions from 7:00 AM during summer. Out of season (November–March), many seasonal businesses close entirely or reduce to weekends only.

content_blocks.title: Cornwall Time Zone — Same as London, GMT/BST
content_blocks.content: Cornwall, England's most south-westerly county, operates on the same time zone as the rest of the United Kingdom: Greenwich Mean Time (GMT/UTC+0) in winter and British Summer Time (BST/UTC+1) from the last Sunday in March to the last Sunday in October. Despite sitting at nearly the same longitude as Dublin and closer to the Atlantic than any other English county, Cornwall has no separate time designation. Land's End, the UK's most westerly mainland point, technically experiences sunrise and sunset slightly later than London, but the clock matches London exactly. Cornwall is 5 hours ahead of New York (EST), 1 hour behind Paris (CET), and shares its time with Dublin and Lisbon during winter months.

content_blocks.title: Cornwall: Celtic Peninsula and Britain's Tourism Jewel
content_blocks.content: Cornwall is one of the UK's most distinctive regions — a Celtic peninsula with its own language (Cornish), distinct culture, and a stunning 422-mile coastline. The county draws over 4 million visitors annually, making tourism its dominant industry. Newquay is the UK's surf capital, hosting the Boardmasters Festival each August. The Eden Project near St Austell — a global landmark of sustainable architecture — attracts 700,000 visitors a year. The Cornish Riviera, centred on towns like St Ives, Mousehole, and Padstow (home of Rick Stein's restaurants), is famous for seafood, art galleries, and cream teas. Cornwall was a key filming location for the BBC's Poldark series, drawing international TV tourists. The county also has a deep mining heritage recognised by its UNESCO World Heritage Status (Cornish Mining Landscape).

content_blocks.title: Visiting and Contacting Cornwall from Overseas
content_blocks.content: For overseas visitors planning a trip to Cornwall, the county lies in the GMT/BST zone, identical to London. From the US East Coast (EST/UTC-5), Cornwall is 5 hours ahead year-round — a 9:00 AM New York morning reaches Cornwall at 2:00 PM, well within business hours. From Australia's east coast (AEST/UTC+10), Cornwall is 10 hours behind in winter, meaning a 7:00 PM Sydney call reaches Cornwall at 9:00 AM GMT. Travel to Cornwall typically involves flying into London Heathrow or Gatwick, then taking the GWR train (approximately 4.5–5 hours to Truro or Penzance) or driving via the M5/A30. Alternatively, Newquay Cornwall Airport offers direct flights from London Gatwick, Manchester, and Dublin. Cornwall's country code is +44 (UK) and Truro (the county town) uses the 01872 area code.

timezone_facts.full_name: Greenwich Mean Time / British Summer Time
timezone_facts.dst_start: Last Sunday in March at 1:00 AM GMT
timezone_facts.dst_end: Last Sunday in October at 2:00 AM BST
